mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9 percent, labor market slack signals severe demand contraction and recessionary pressures, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0, or the signal loses predictive power during post-2009 recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting consumer spending, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth decouples from FEDFUNDS stability, or interaction term IC is negative over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds historical averages.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 revisions show zero correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 225, inflationary pressures force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL breaching 225, or the threshold fails to predict negative returns during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized labor market and construction sector expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when housing starts rise while payroll growth stagnates, or the interaction effect yields negative IC over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment signals deteriorating economic activity and earnings recession risks, driving negative short-term equity returns during contraction phases.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns when industrial production declines are offset by strong services sector data, or predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent in backtests.
